title: The One with the Donor

jokes.text: Oh, that feels so good. / Oh, lucky me. Coffee and a live sex show.
jokes.setup: Someone expressing pleasure from what sounds like a sexual context
jokes.punchline: Second character assumes they're witnessing something inappropriate when it's innocent
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection opening - efficient setup/payoff structure with good economy

jokes.text: I was just reading to Emma. / From Cosmo?
jokes.setup: Parent claiming to read to baby
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate magazine choice for infant
jokes.explanation: Sharp two-word punchline with excellent economy - absurd juxtaposition of baby and adult magazine

jokes.text: Climax Your Way to Better Skin
jokes.setup: Reading magazine article title to baby
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre in child-inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Relies on obvious sexual pun but works in context of reading to baby

jokes.text: Not me. Not me. Not me.
jokes.punchline: Character desperately avoiding responsibility
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor showing self-awareness about lack of fashion sense

jokes.text: and to always, always, always put on underwear when trying on clothes
jokes.punchline: Awkward personal hygiene detail shared publicly
jokes.explanation: TMI moment that's relatable and uncomfortable - good escalation from fashion to hygiene

jokes.text: And some slutty lingerie. Slutty!
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic emphasis on inappropriate addition
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from wholesome to inappropriate with enthusiastic delivery

jokes.text: Pain in the ass! / That's off, right?
jokes.punchline: Realizes they might still be on speakerphone
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - immediate consequence for bad behavior with efficient execution

jokes.text: Oh, isn't that nice? The three of you, trying on slutty lingerie together.
jokes.punchline: Imagining inappropriate group activity
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the previous slutty lingerie joke with character's fantasy

jokes.text: That's not what we're gonna do. / Why'd you ruin it? Who was I hurting?
jokes.punchline: Disappointment at losing fantasy
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - shows harmless perversion with self-aware delivery

jokes.text: I've always tried so hard to be a good hostess.
jokes.setup: Doctor says uterus is inhospitable environment
jokes.punchline: Monica relates medical term to her hostess skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica joke - takes medical terminology and filters through her obsession with hosting. Clever wordplay with excellent character integration.

jokes.text: While I was growing up, they sure seemed to be in a hurry to get places.
jokes.punchline: Reference to teenage sexual urgency/premature ejaculation
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion - takes medical issue and finds unexpected angle about adolescence

jokes.text: Don't worry. After a while, you'll tune it out.
jokes.punchline: Doctor treats it as routine annoyance
jokes.explanation: Professional deadpan delivery - doctor's matter-of-fact response to awkwardness

jokes.text: Is that a hint? Because we love you, Dr. Connelly, but we don't want you to be our child.
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets suggestion as doctor offering herself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - takes normal adoption suggestion and finds absurd interpretation

jokes.text: Wow! Talk about an inhospitable environment.
jokes.punchline: Doctor uses Monica's earlier line against them
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that gives doctor a comeback using their own terminology

jokes.text: Not gonna find any clothes in there.
jokes.setup: Someone heading out to go shopping
jokes.punchline: Obvious statement about wrong location
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor - efficient but not particularly clever

jokes.text: When was the last time he made a submission deadline for an abstract?
jokes.punchline: Academic-specific insult about deadlines
jokes.explanation: Very Ross-specific humor about academic world - good character integration but niche appeal

jokes.text: Just seeing what it'd be like to be a paleontologist. / It's fun. Yeah.
jokes.punchline: Treats complex career like a game
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey moment - his childlike approach to adult situations

jokes.text: You're not gonna to do a magic trick, are you? / No.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's concern and Ross's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Ross's history of inappropriate presentation choices

jokes.text: I think I can safely say that we all have family issues, work and/or are sick.
jokes.setup: Ross inviting everyone to paleontology conference
jokes.punchline: Universal excuse covering all possible reasons to decline
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler line - comprehensive excuse that covers everyone efficiently

jokes.text: But you come first. / I'm there. / I don't know Ross, I'm not feeling too.. / Barbados is in the Caribbean, Joe.
jokes.setup: Friends showing loyalty vs. making excuses
jokes.punchline: Joey's instant attitude change upon learning location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - shallow but honest priorities clearly established

jokes.text: Why didn't anybody say so?
jokes.setup: Learning conference is in tropical location
jokes.punchline: Joey's indignation at buried lede
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke - Joey's priorities fully revealed

jokes.text: Apparently, you only get porn if you're giving a sperm sample.
jokes.setup: Fertility clinic visit wasn't as fun as last time
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate expectation about medical facility amenities
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler TMI moment with medical setting awkwardness

jokes.text: Enough said. I'm there for you, man. Where is she, upstairs?
jokes.punchline: Joey volunteers himself inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey misunderstanding - helpful intention with completely inappropriate execution

jokes.text: Your long-standing offer to have sex with my wife is appreciated.
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry acknowledgment of pattern
jokes.explanation: Good series callback delivered with Chandler's trademark dryness

jokes.text: What?! No! I'm not gonna give them Ben!
jokes.setup: Joey suggesting Ross help with baby situation
jokes.punchline: Ross thinks Joey means giving away his existing child
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Ross's protective parental instinct creates absurd interpretation

jokes.text: Well, that's not what you want.
jokes.setup: Ross realizes his mind-boggling presentation put professor to sleep
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of presentation failure
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement - Ross's academic disaster reduced to mild concern

jokes.text: When I go to the party later.. Mike will know I'm over him because I'll smell like another guy.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre logic about fake relationship evidence
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe logic - convoluted plan with strange reasoning that somehow makes sense for her

jokes.text: Okay, good, I'm dating a Russian cabdriver.
jokes.punchline: Specific stereotype from scent
jokes.explanation: Efficient stereotype joke with good specificity

jokes.text: Seriously, does anyone buy this? I smell like beets.
jokes.punchline: Unappetizing food comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Russian cabdriver to specific unpleasant scent

jokes.text: On Melanie Griffith in Working Girl.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about shoulder pad jackets
jokes.punchline: Dated fashion reference showing how passé the style is
jokes.explanation: Sharp fashion burn with specific pop culture reference - efficient putdown

jokes.text: Because I've seen them. / You've seen all the movies? / Yeah. I'm a big fan. / Of the movies. / You know, motion pictures. / The talkies.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward over-explanation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing character digging deeper hole with each explanation

jokes.text: You know, just the way she waltzed in here all smart.. and tall, you know.. and just swept Joey off his feet. Nobody else has a chance.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous complaints including height
jokes.explanation: Shows Phoebe's jealousy through petty complaints about positive traits

jokes.text: You know, Monica's mom.
jokes.setup: Listing who else could have chance with Joey
jokes.punchline: Including inappropriate older woman
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation to absurd choice shows Phoebe's scattered thinking

jokes.text: Wow. Isn't that ironic that he liked you, and now you like..? / I get it!
jokes.setup: Rachel starting to explain ironic situation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe cuts off obvious observation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing character impatience with obvious statement

jokes.text: Any chance Charlie has a deaf twin?
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to avoid smell while liking Joey
jokes.punchline: Bizarre solution combining multiple problems
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe logic - combines beet smell problem with Joey crush in impossibly specific solution

jokes.text: Look. He's intelligent, he's healthy, he's athletic. I mean, he's sperm-tastic!
jokes.punchline: Made-up portmanteau combining sperm and fantastic
jokes.explanation: Good invented word that captures Chandler's enthusiasm about uncomfortable topic

jokes.text: Come up. Meet my wife. Give us your sperm.
jokes.setup: Monica asking what he said to Zack
jokes.punchline: Blunt summary of inappropriate dinner invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with maximum economy - reduces complex situation to absurd directness

jokes.text: I mean, even if my sperm worked fine, I think he'd be the way to go.
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggesting replacement of himself
jokes.explanation: Dark self-deprecating humor showing Chandler's insecurities about masculinity

jokes.text: Do you have a coaster? I don't want to make a ring. / Tell me about yourself, Zack.
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately interrogating him
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition between Zack's politeness and Monica's intensity

jokes.text: Oh, thank God, I can't hear a word that you're saying. / I didn't say anything yet.
jokes.punchline: Premature celebration
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure with efficient misdirection

jokes.text: It is annoying when parents put babies on the phone. / All right, enough out of you!
jokes.setup: Rachel's awkward attempt to distract from eavesdropping
jokes.punchline: Snapping at imaginary baby
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's panic leading to inappropriate response

jokes.text: What? But I already bought my ticket to Bermuda! / Barbados. / Fine, I'll rent a car and drive.
jokes.punchline: Absurd solution to drive to Caribbean island
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - geographical ignorance plus determination equals impossible solution

jokes.text: In fact, he was just talking in his sleep before.. and evidently he wants someone named Fran to 'spank him harder.'
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sleep talking revelation
jokes.explanation: Adds uncomfortable sexual detail to already awkward situation

jokes.text: I mean, hey, Shakespeare, how about a chase scene once in a while?
jokes.setup: Joey fell asleep during Shakespeare audition
jokes.punchline: Critique of classical literature lacking action
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - treats Shakespeare like a Hollywood screenplay writer, showing his limited cultural perspective with perfect character voice

jokes.text: What do you think? I want his genes for my kid. Those eyes, those cheekbones. / Okay, there's enthusiastic, and there's just plain gay.
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out inappropriate level of male appreciation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler crossing line from practical to attracted

jokes.text: No. Although, I did have an uncle who voted for Dukakis.
jokes.setup: Asked about family mental illness history
jokes.punchline: Treats political choice as mental defect
jokes.explanation: Period-specific political humor with good misdirection from medical to political

jokes.text: You know what's not funny? Male pattern baldness.
jokes.setup: Zack wanting to tell a joke to lighten mood
jokes.punchline: Monica continuing inappropriate medical screening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - unable to stop screening mode even when guest tries to be social

jokes.text: Did you have braces as a child? / No, I didn't. / Yes!
jokes.punchline: Monica's celebration of good genetic news
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's intensity about finding perfect genetic material

jokes.text: We're teeth people, Zack.
jokes.setup: Monica's obsession with Zack's dental history
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact explanation of bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler line - treats absurd situation as completely normal with maximum economy

jokes.text: I don't have time for your childish games, okay? I still have to find something incredible to wear.. so I can beat Mike at 'Who's More Over Who'!
jokes.punchline: Immediately describing her own childish competition
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Phoebe's self-awareness combined with complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Yeah, that's Phoebe. That's Phoebe. You know, she just wants them all. It's, like, she's nympho.
jokes.setup: Trying to cover for overheard conversation
jokes.punchline: Rachel making up increasingly inappropriate explanation
jokes.explanation: Shows Rachel digging herself deeper with inappropriate lies about friend

jokes.text: stop-eating hot!
jokes.punchline: Made-up superlative combining food and attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Creative invented phrase that efficiently conveys extreme attractiveness

jokes.text: That's a very serious thing in my culture.
jokes.setup: Not wanting to lose face to Mike
jokes.punchline: Phoebe claiming cultural tradition for personal vanity
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe - inventing cultural justification for personal behavior

jokes.text: But not on the wine that you made, okay? Because I just don't want to go back to the emergency room.
jokes.setup: Rachel offering to get Phoebe drunk
jokes.punchline: Reference to Rachel's dangerous homemade wine
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established running gag about Rachel's domestic failures

jokes.text: This is a hundred-thousand-ruble note, which is worth approximately.. nothing.
jokes.punchline: Worthless due to exchange rate/inflation
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with economic humor about post-Soviet currency

jokes.text: Well, after eight years of research, I discovered.. that it can't be done.
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of career failure
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of scientific career disaster

jokes.text: To people who base eight years of research on a typo in a scientific journal.
jokes.setup: Explaining that research failures happen in science
jokes.punchline: Reveals his mistake was preventable error
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - turns professional setback into personal incompetence

jokes.text: Because I'm not seeing anybody. I just totally made that up. / Really? / I don't know why, I'm sorry. I guess I just didn't want to lose face.
jokes.punchline: Admits to same face-saving lie as Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing both characters have same insecurity

jokes.text: Hey, there's Phoebe. Is that Mike she's with? / No, that's David. / There's a third guy? / Tip of the iceberg.
jokes.setup: Seeing Phoebe with yet another man
jokes.punchline: Joey suggesting even more men in her life
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier misunderstanding about Phoebe's love life

jokes.text: Are you always tired? Because that could be a sign of clinical depression.
jokes.setup: Zack saying he's tired and wants to leave
jokes.punchline: Chandler continuing medical screening inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler can't stop screening mode even as guest leaves

jokes.text: Why not? Just because his great-grandmother was obese? Our kid's gonna get that from you anyway.
jokes.setup: Monica rejecting Zack as sperm donor
jokes.punchline: Chandler insulting wife's genetics
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler inappropriate comment during serious moment

jokes.text: Yeah, he's better! / No, he's not. / If I can't get pregnant with you, then I don't want to get pregnant by him.. or anyone else.
jokes.setup: Chandler insisting Zack is better choice
jokes.punchline: Monica's romantic rejection of practical solution
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Monica's priorities

jokes.text: I'm the husband. I'm supposed to bring the sperm.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why he doesn't want sperm donor
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex emotions to basic marital duty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - reduces emotional complexity to practical job description

jokes.text: And I wanna mess it up in our own specific way.
jokes.setup: Monica wanting to raise adopted child with Chandler
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging they'll damage child but uniquely
jokes.explanation: Excellent line combining parental love with self-awareness about their flaws

jokes.text: Wait. If we're lucky, and we're really, really, really quiet.. we may be able to hear the sound of a condom breaking.
jokes.setup: Suggesting their future baby is being conceived somewhere
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate specific detail about conception circumstances
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - takes sweet moment and adds inappropriate realistic detail

jokes.text: You wouldn't know if Jeanette's planning on keeping her baby, would you?
jokes.punchline: Immediately violating boundaries again
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing Chandler can't help himself despite self-awareness

tmdb_overview: Rachel and Phoebe take Joey's girlfriend Charlie shopping, where she overhears the two of them gossiping about Joey. Monica and Chandler consider alternate pregnancy options.
